title: Ornithogalum angolense
intro: Ornithogalum angolense, also known as the Angolan star-of-Bethlehem, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family, native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ornithogalum angolense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ornithogalum angolense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make tinctures and ointments, although its use is not well-studied in modern medicine and it should be used with caution.
taxonomy: Ornithogalum angolense is classified within the family Asparagaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the asparagus or lily family. This classification places Ornithogalum angolense among other similar plants such as agaves, hyacinths, and asparagus. The genus Ornithogalum is a group of bulbous perennials native to Africa and southern Europe. Ornithogalum angolense is native to Angola, a country in southern Africa, and is a part of this diverse and widespread genus. The Asparagaceae family is a significant group of flowering plants that includes many species with ornamental value.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
